ddls:
CREATE TABLE "records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the record */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was created */,
  "created_on_behalf_by" INTEGER /* ID of the delegate user who created the record on behalf of another user */,
  "logical_name" TEXT /* Logical name of the record */,
  "schema_name" TEXT /* Schema name of the record */,
  "display_name" TEXT /* Display name of the record */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the record (e.g., Lookup, String, DateTime) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the record */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department associated with the record */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the record (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the record */,
  "last_modified_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was last modified */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the record has been logically deleted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_records_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_records_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_records_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_records_created_on_behalf_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_on_behalf_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, manager, end-user) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the user belongs to */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "attributes" (
  "attribute_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attribute */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record the attribute belongs to */,
  "logical_name" TEXT /* Logical name of the attribute */,
  "schema_name" TEXT /* Schema name of the attribute */,
  "display_name" TEXT /* Display name of the attribute */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the attribute (e.g., Lookup, String, DateTime) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attribute */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the attribute */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the attribute was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the attribute */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is required */,
  "is_read_only" INTEGER /* Indicates if the attribute is read-only */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the attribute */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attribute_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attributes_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES records ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_attributes_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record affected */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Date and time when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details about the action performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent (e.g., browser) used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES records ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "delegate_users" (
  "delegate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each delegate user */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is being delegated to */,
  "delegate_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the delegate user */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the delegation starts */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the delegation ends */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the delegation (e.g., active, expired) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("delegate_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_delegate_users_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_delegate_users_delegate_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("delegate_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the department was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (JSON or comma-separated list) */,
  "created_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assigned_on" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
